reference_impl: official Boltz-2 (torch + pytorch-lightning, CPU -- qb1; vast.ai GPU rental infeasible this run, ~114 KB/s pypi)

settings.msa_source: local ColabFold DB (~/.boltz/msa_db: UniRef30 2302 + EnvDB 202108, same DBs as the remote ColabFold server)
settings.execution_device: CPU (qb1) -- GPU rental infeasible (slow pypi); reference is hardware-invariant per the no-MSA GPU-vs-CPU check in this benchmark

msa: local ColabFold DB MSA, committed at docs/pharma-benchmark-data/ref-fixtures/boltz2/affinity_dhfr/msa-colabfold_200step_5affsample_3recycle_bf16_mwcorr_gpu/msa.a3m, fed to boltz via yaml msa: field (no network)
provenance: Generated 2026-07-21 on qb1 CPU with official boltz 2.2.1, feeding the committed local-DB MSA (msa.a3m). Same impl + kernel (torch-einsum triangle path, the CPU default) as the committed no-MSA CPU fixtures, only the MSA differs. The no-MSA GPU-vs-CPU check in this benchmark (boltz2-cpu-vs-gpu-ref.json) proved the Boltz-2 reference is hardware-invariant, so this CPU MSA reference represents what a GPU pharma user would see.
invalidation_rule: Regenerate ONLY if the pinned reference_commit/version or settings change. For any other change the fixture is reused and only the device side re-runs.
